title: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Announces Pricing of Remarketed New York State Environmental Facilities Corporation Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds
author: Na
description: Casella Waste Systems priced a remarketing of $15.0 million in New York State Environmental Facilities Corporation Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds. The bonds will be remarketed on June 1, 2026 at a new interest rate of 4.300% per annum for a 10-year period ending June 1, 2036. The bonds are guaranteed by substantially all of Casella's subsidiaries.
keywords: New York State Environmental Facilities Corporation

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement is a routine debt refinancing activity with a favorable interest rate of 4.300%. While this represents successful capital management, it is a standard operational matter without indication of significant business growth or challenges. The company successfully completed the remarketing as planned, which is neither particularly positive nor negative for the business outlook.

title: Clairvest Announces the Sale of Star Waste Systems to Casella Waste Systems, Inc.
author: Clairvest Group Inc.
description: Clairvest Group Inc. announced the sale of Star Waste Systems to Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Clairvest received approximately US$38 million in proceeds and realized a positive impact of approximately $2.00 per share on book value. Star Waste, a leading solid waste collection and recycling provider in the Greater Boston Area, was grown significantly over four years with eight acquisitions and 3x revenue growth. This marks Clairvest's eighth successful realization in the environmental services sector.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Acquirer of Star Waste Systems. No specific financial details or strategic implications provided in the article regarding Casella's perspective or performance impact from this acquisition.

title: Clairvest Announces the Sale of Star Waste Systems to Casella Waste Systems, Inc.
author: Na
description: Clairvest Group Inc. and Clairvest Equity Partners VI have successfully exited their investment in Star Waste Systems by selling their interests to Casella Waste Systems, Inc. CVG received approximately US$38 million in sale proceeds, generating a positive impact of approximately $2.00 per share on book value. Over four years, Star Waste completed eight acquisitions and grew revenue by over 3.0x, becoming a strategically significant waste management platform in the Greater Boston market.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Acquiring Star Waste to expand its waste management platform in the Greater Boston market. No financial details or strategic implications disclosed for Casella, limiting sentiment assessment to neutral.

title: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Completes Acquisition of Star Waste Systems
author: Not Specified
description: Casella Waste Systems completed its acquisition of Star Waste Systems on April 1, 2026, adding approximately $100 million in annualized revenue. The deal includes three solid waste collection locations and a C&D processing facility in the Boston area and southern New Hampshire. Year-to-date, Casella has acquired four businesses totaling approximately $150 million in annualized revenues.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company successfully completed a strategic acquisition that enhances its market position in the attractive Boston area, adds $100 million in annualized revenue, and represents strong execution with four acquisitions year-to-date totaling $150 million in revenues. Management expressed confidence in continued growth.

title: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Announces Pricing of Remarketed New York State Environmental Facilities Corporation Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds
author: Casella Waste Systems, Inc.
description: Casella Waste Systems is remarketing $37.5 million in Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds, with $2.5 million to be redeemed, at a new 4.250% interest rate for a five-year period ending September 2030.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is executing a standard financial refinancing of existing bonds with no significant positive or negative implications, maintaining a routine financial transaction
